rationale: Adjudication boundary, conflicts, and limitations: this run acted only as the final adjudicator for frozen candidate 49ae5e2151a6dbed7ba20bbb2aac34eecdaa0039. The accountable principal github:VSBDev is also the lesson author, PR author, and disclosed operator of the review agents; that overlap is permitted by the active founding-stage policy but is a declared governance limitation. Agent providers, models, run IDs, versions, and instruction digests are disclosed and attested rather than independently verified. This adjudication did not perform a new academic, learning-design, accessibility, or legal review. Its instructions digest covers the exact 1,700-byte UTF-8 user adjudication assignment with no trailing newline and excludes unavailable hidden provider and platform instructions. Candidate and review eligibility: the candidate exists, is a production Lesson Format v1/schema v3 pack for PREM-LPP-003 version 0.1.0 at standard risk, and is an ancestor of worktree and PR head d04e8e2941dfce649c44b488fa0c3fb23a7a8ff7. No lesson-pack content changed after freezing; only the three review artifacts and deterministic generated site outputs were added. Each counted artifact is schema-valid, approves the exact candidate and identity, has no unresolved major or blocking finding, declares no conflict, uses a unique non-author run, and exactly matches a current COMMENTED GitHub review submitted by VSBDev with commit_id set to the candidate (GitHub review IDs 4731665492, 4731666997, and 4731671752). The eligible set satisfies standard-lesson-v2: three approvals, one per required role, one principal, three distinct review runs, and three distinct declared provider/model families (Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI). Numerical quorum is necessary but not sufficient. Revision is required because every one of the six retained claims remains pending-review and lesson.json sourceConfidence remains pending-review, while the adjudication template requires reviewed retained claims and resolved source confidence for a merge decision. The academic review also identifies three unresolved minor defects that are present in the frozen text: scene-diagnostic is declared no-material-claims despite learner-facing mastery-evidence assertions and has no same-scene claim/source note; the worked-example source note calls variation an evidence-supported default without a mapped variation claim or a locator that includes the cited synthesis's variation evidence; and the Ari baseline/day-0 link counts are ambiguous. These are remediable, but the first two directly implicate the mandatory claim-evidence contract and cannot be waived by an approving verdict. The accessibility-and-rights review additionally records a nonblocking guided-frame keyboard-scroll defect; the continuous Reading mode supplies an equivalent route, so this does not independently require rejection. There is no dissenting verdict. The three approvals and their limitations remain part of the public record, but they do not resolve defects that are unchanged in the candidate. Because correcting lesson content, claims, or references will stale all current approvals, the proper decision is revise rather than merge or reject.
conditions: Create a new frozen candidate that either maps the diagnostic scene's mastery-evidence assertions to claim-mastery-performance with a same-scene source note or rewrites them as genuinely non-evidentiary routing instructions.

Correct the worked-example evidence mapping by adding support and an exact locator for meaningful variation or by narrowing the source note, and clarify how Ari's new day-0 attempt relates to the two-link baseline.
Resolve every retained claim's reviewStatus and the lesson-level sourceConfidence through the new candidate's governance record; do not treat a metadata-only assertion as a substitute for review.

Freeze a new candidate commit after the revisions, repeat the academic, learning-design, and accessibility-rights reviews against that same new commit with eligible GitHub submissions, and obtain a fresh adjudication run because the current approvals will be stale.
